# KePost: Post-processing for dMRI derivatives of the Strauss Neuroplasticity Brain Bank (SNBB)

# Overview

```
.. list-table::
    :stub-columns: 1

    * - docs
      - |docs|
    * - tests, CI & coverage
      - |github-actions| |codecov| |codacy|
    * - codeclimate
      - |codeclimate-maintainability| |codeclimate-testcoverage|
    * - version
      - |pypi| |python|
    * - styling
      - |black| |isort| |flake8| |pre-commit|
    * - license
      - |license|
```

KePost is a Python package that provides post-processing tools for dMRI derivatives of the Strauss Neuroplasticity Brain Bank (SNBB).

- Free software: MIT license
- Documentation: <https://kepost.readthedocs.io>.

## Features

- TODO

## Credits

This package was created with [Cookiecutter] and the [audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage] project template.

[audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage
[cookiecutter]: https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.mrtrix3.utils.MRConvert
documentation:
Perform conversion between different file types and optionally extract a subset of the input image.

# Example

```pycon
>>> import nipype.interfaces.mrtrix3 as mrt
>>> mrconvert = mrt.MRConvert()
>>> mrconvert.inputs.in_file = 'dwi.nii.gz'
>>> mrconvert.inputs.grad_fsl = ('bvecs', 'bvals')
>>> mrconvert.cmdline                             # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
'mrconvert -fslgrad bvecs bvals dwi.nii.gz dwi.mif'
>>> mrconvert.run()                               # doctest: +SKIP
```

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.mrtrix3.connectivity.BuildConnectome
documentation:
Build a connectome from a streamlines tractogram

# Example

```pycon
>>> import nipype.interfaces.mrtrix3 as mrt
>>> connectome = mrt.BuildConnectome()
>>> connectome.inputs.in_tracts = 'tracks.tck'
>>> connectome.inputs.in_nodes = 'nodes.nii.gz'
>>> connectome.cmdline                               # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
'tck2connectome tracks.tck nodes.nii.gz connectome.csv'
>>> connectome.run()                                 # doctest: +SKIP
```

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.mrtrix3.tracking.TckSift2
documentation:
Optimise per-streamline cross-section multipliers to match
a whole-brain tractogram to fixel-wise fibre densities

# Example

```pycon
>>> import nipype.interfaces.mrtrix3 as mrt
>>> sift = mrt.TCKSift2()
>>> sift.inputs.in_file = 'tracks.tck'
>>> sift.inputs.in_fod = 'fod.mif'
>>> sift.cmdline
'tcksift2 tracks.tck fod.mif sift2_weights.txt'
>>> sift.run()  # doctest: +SKIP
```

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.mrtrix3.tracking.TckSift
documentation:
Select the streamlines from a tractogram that are most consistent with the
underlying FOD image.

# Example

```pycon
>>> import nipype.interfaces.mrtrix3 as mrt
>>> sift = mrt.TCKSift()
>>> sift.inputs.in_file = 'tracks.tck'
>>> sift.inputs.in_fod = 'fod.mif'
>>> sift.cmdline
'tcksift tracks.tck fod.mif sift.tck'
>>> sift.run()  # doctest: +SKIP
```

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.mrtrix3.tracking.TckMap
documentation:
Generate a track-weighted image using the provided tractogram

# Example

```pycon
>>> import nipype.interfaces.mrtrix3 as mrt
>>> tckmap = mrt.TckMap()
>>> tckmap.inputs.in_file = 'tracks.tck'
>>> tckmap.inputs.contrast = 'tdi'
>>> tckmap.inputs.template = 'template.mif'
>>> tckmap.cmdline
'tckmap -contrast tdi -template template.mif tracks.tck out.mif'
>>> tckmap.run()  # doctest: +SKIP
```

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.bids.bids._BIDSDataGrabberInputSpec
documentation: Saves the ``in_file`` into a BIDS-Derivatives folder provided
When multiple source files are passed, only common entities are passed down.
For example, if two T1w images from different sessions are used to generate
a single image, the session entity is removed automatically.
If, on the other hand, only one is used, the session is preserved:
"""Initialize the SimpleInterface and extend inputs with custom entities."""
# Add additional input fields (self.inputs is an object)
# Read in the dictionary of metadata
# inputs passed in construction take priority
# Initialize entities with those from the source file.
if all(ent.get(k) == v for ent in in_entities[1:])
# Override extension with that of the input file(s)
out_entities["extension"][i] = f"{ext}.gz" if compress[i] else ext
# Override entities with those set as inputs
if value is not None and isdefined(value):
# Clean up native resolution with space
raise ValueError(f"Could not build path with entities {out_entities}.")
# Make sure the interpolated values is embedded in a list, and check
for i, (orig_file, dest_file) in enumerate(zip(in_file, dest_files)):
# Set data and header iff changes need to be made. If these are
# still None when it's time to write, just copy.
[None if u == "unknown" else u for u in hdr.get_xyzt_units()]
# Default to mm, use sec if data type is bold
xcodes = (1, 1)  # Derivative in its original scanner space
if curr_codes != xcodes or curr_units != units:
f"Could not get data type of file {self.inputs.source_file[0]}"
# coerce dataobj to new data dtype
if new_data is new_header is None:
# Without this, we would be writing nans
# This is our punishment for hacking around nibabel defaults
# For dtseries, we have been generating weird non-BIDS JSON files.
# We can safely keep producing them to avoid breaking derivatives, but
# only the existing keys should keep going into them.
# The future: the extension is the first . and everything after

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.bids.bids.BIDSDataGrabber
documentation:
Collect files from a BIDS directory structure.

```
.. testsetup::

    >>> data_dir_canary()
```

```pycon
>>> bids_src = BIDSDataGrabber(anat_only=False)
>>> bids_src.inputs.subject_data = bids_collect_data(
...     str(datadir / 'ds114'), '01', bids_validate=False)[0]
>>> bids_src.inputs.subject_id = '01'
>>> res = bids_src.run()
>>> res.outputs.t1w  # doctest: +ELLIPSIS +NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE
['.../ds114/sub-01/ses-retest/anat/sub-01_ses-retest_T1w.nii.gz',
 '.../ds114/sub-01/ses-test/anat/sub-01_ses-test_T1w.nii.gz']
```

functions.name: kepost.interfaces.reports.viz.plot_overlay
documentation:
Generate a static mosaic with ROIs represented by their delimiting contour.

Plot segmentation as contours over the image (e.g. anatomical).
seg_niis should be a list of files. mask_nii helps determine the cut
coordinates. plot_params will be passed on to nilearn plot_* functions. If
seg_niis is a list of size one, it behaves as if it was plotting the mask.

classes.name: kepost.interfaces.dipy.reconst.ReconstDTI
documentation:
Calculates the diffusion tensor model parameters
Example
\-------
\>>> import nipype.interfaces.dipy as dipy
\>>> dti = dipy.DTI()
\>>> dti.inputs.in_file = 'diffusion.nii'
\>>> dti.inputs.in_bvec = 'bvecs'
\>>> dti.inputs.in_bval = 'bvals'
\>>> dti.run() # doctest: +SKIP

modules.name: kepost.config
documentation:
A Python module to maintain unique, run-wide *kepost* settings.
This module implements the memory structures to keep a consistent, singleton config.
Settings are passed across processes via filesystem, and a copy of the settings for
each run and subject is left under
`<output_dir>/sub-<participant_id>/log/<run_unique_id>/kepost.toml`.
Settings are stored using {abbr}`ToML (Tom's Markup Language)`.
The module has a {py:func}`~kepost.config.to_filename` function to allow writing out
the settings to hard disk in *ToML* format, which looks like:
.. literalinclude:: ../kepost/data/tests/config.toml

> ```
> language
>
> toml
>
> name
>
> kepost.toml
>
> caption
>
> Example file representation of kepostrep settings.
> ```

This config file is used to pass the settings across processes,
using the {py:func}`~kepost.config.load` function.
Configuration sections
\----------------------
.. autoclass:: environment

> ```
> members
>
> ```

```
.. autoclass:: execution
   :members:
```

```
.. autoclass:: workflow
   :members:
```

```
.. autoclass:: nipype
   :members:
```

# Usage

A config file is used to pass settings and collect information as the execution
graph is built across processes.
.. code-block:: Python

> from kepost import config
> config_file = config.execution.work_dir / '.kepost.toml'
> config.to_filename(config_file)
> \# Call build_workflow(config_file, retval) in a subprocess
> with Manager() as mgr:
>
> > from .workflow import build_workflow
> > retval = mgr.dict()
> > p = Process(target=build_workflow, args=(str(config_file), retval))
> > p.start()
> > p.join()
>
> config.load(config_file)
> \# Access configs from any code section as:
> value = config.section.setting

# Logging

```
.. autoclass:: loggers
   :members:
```

# Other responsibilities

The {py:mod}`config` is responsible for other conveniency actions.
: - Switching Python's {obj}`multiprocessing` to *forkserver* mode.
  - Set up a filter for warnings as early as possible.
  - Automated I/O magic operations. Some conversions need to happen in the
    store/load processes (e.g., from/to {obj}`~pathlib.Path` \<-> {obj}`str`,
    {py:class}`~bids.layout.BIDSLayout`, etc.)

functions.name: kepost.config._set_ants_seed
documentation: Fix random seed for antsRegistration, antsAI, antsMotionCorr

functions.name: kepost.config._set_numpy_seed
documentation: NumPy's random seed is independent from Python's `random` module

functions.name: kepost.config.from_dict
documentation:
Read settings from a flat dictionary.
Arguments
\---------
setting : dict

> Settings to apply to any configuration

init

: Initialize all, none, or a subset of configurations.

ignore

: Collection of keys in `setting` to ignore

functions.name: kepost.config.load
documentation:
Load settings from file.
Arguments
\---------
filename : {py:class}`os.PathLike`

> TOML file containing fMRIPrep configuration.

skip

: Sets of values to ignore during load, keyed by section name

init

: Initialize all, none, or a subset of configurations.

classes.name: kepost.config.environment

class_variables.name: kepost.config.environment.exec_env
documentation: A string representing the execution platform.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.environment.overcommit_policy
documentation: Linux's kernel virtual memory overcommit policy.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.environment.overcommit_limit
documentation: Linux's kernel virtual memory overcommit limits.

Read-only options regarding the platform and environment.
Crawls runtime descriptive settings (e.g., default FreeSurfer license,
execution environment, nipype and *kepost* versions, etc.).
The `environment` section is not loaded in from file,
only written out when settings are exported.
This config section is useful when reporting issues,
and these variables are tracked whenever the user does not
opt-out using the `--notrack` argument.

methods.name: kepost.config.nipype.get_plugin
documentation: Format a dictionary for Nipype consumption.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.nipype.crashfile_format
documentation: The file format for crashfiles, either text or pickle.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.nipype.get_linked_libs
documentation: Run NiPype's tool to enlist linked libraries for every interface.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.nipype.memory_gb
documentation: Estimation in GB of the RAM this workflow can allocate at any given time.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.nipype.nprocs
documentation: Number of processes (compute tasks) that can be run in parallel (multiprocessing only).

class_variables.name: kepost.config.nipype.omp_nthreads
documentation: Number of CPUs a single process can access for multithreaded execution.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.nipype.stop_on_first_crash
documentation: Whether the workflow should stop or continue after the first error.

methods.name: kepost.config.execution.init
documentation: Create a new BIDS Layout accessible with {attr}`~execution.layout`.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.keprep_dir
documentation: An existing path to the dataset, which must be an output of KePrep.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.keprep_database_dir
documentation: Path to the directory containing SQLite database indices for the input KePrep dataset.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.fs_license_file
documentation: An existing file containing a FreeSurfer license.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.log_dir
documentation: The path to a directory that contains execution logs.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.low_mem
documentation: Utilize uncompressed NIfTIs and other tricks to minimize memory allocation.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.output_dir
documentation: Folder where derivatives will be stored.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.run_uuid
documentation: Unique identifier of this particular run.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.participant_label
documentation: List of participant identifiers that are to be preprocessed.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.work_dir
documentation: Path to a working directory where intermediate results will be available.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.execution.write_graph
documentation: Write out the computational graph corresponding to the planned preprocessing.

classes.name: kepost.config.workflow

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.five_tissue_type_algorithm
documentation: Algorithm to use for the generation of the five-tissue-type image. Available algorithms are: `hsvs`, `fsl`.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.gm_probseg_threshold
documentation: Threshold for the probabilistic segmentation of the gray matter.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.atlases
documentation: Parcellation atlas(es) to use for the parcellation step. Available atlases are: `all`, `fan2016`, `huang2022`, `schaefer2018_{n_regions}_{n_networks}`.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.tensor_max_bval
documentation: Maximum b-value to consider for tensor estimation.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.dipy_reconstruction_method
documentation: Reconstruction method to use for the estimation of tensor-derived parameters using dipy.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.dipy_reconstruction_sigma
documentation: Sigma parameter for the RESTORE algorithm. If none provided, sigma will be estimated.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.parcellate_gm
documentation: Whether to apply gray matter masking to atlases prior to parcellation.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.response_algorithm
documentation: Algorithm to estimate the response function.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.fod_algorithm
documentation: Algorithm to estimate the fiber orientation distribution.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.n_raw_tracts
documentation: Number of streamlines to generate in the tractography.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.n_tracts
documentation: Number of streamlines to keep after filtering.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.tracking_max_angle
documentation: Maximum angle between steps in the tractography.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.tracking_lenscale_min
documentation: Minimum length scale for the tractography.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.tracking_lenscale_max
documentation: Maximum length scale for the tractography.

class_variables.name: kepost.config.workflow.fs_scale_gm
documentation: Heuristically downsize the fibre density estimates based on the presence of GM in the voxel.
Configure the particular execution graph of this workflow.

methods.name: kepost.config.loggers.init
documentation:
Set the log level, initialize all loggers into {py:class}`loggers`.
: - Add new logger levels (25: IMPORTANT, and 15: VERBOSE).
  - Add a new sub-logger (`cli`).
  - Logger configuration.

classes.name: kepost.config.seeds

class_variables.name: kepost.config.seeds.master
documentation: Master random seed to initialize the Pseudorandom Number Generator (PRNG)

class_variables.name: kepost.config.seeds.ants
documentation: Seed used for antsRegistration, antsAI, antsMotionCorr
Initialize the PRNG and track random seed assignments

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.eddy_qc.init_eddyqc_wf
documentation: Initialize the eddy quality control workflow

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.utils.calculate_strip_score
documentation:
Calculate the striping score of a given NIfTI file along a given axis.

Parameters
----------
input_file : Union[str, Path]
    The input NIfTI file
axis : int, optional
    The axis along which to calculate the striping score, by default 2
target_freq : float, optional
    The target frequency to detect striping, by default 0.25
freq_tolerance : float, optional
    The frequency tolerance to detect striping, by default 0.05

Returns
-------
list
    The striping scores

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.utils.parse_pct_b_outliers
documentation:
Parse the percentage of outliers per b value.

Parameters
----------
pcts : list
    The list of percentages

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.utils.get
documentation: Get the value from the dictionary

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.utils.parse_eddyqc
documentation: Parse the eddy quality control string

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.tractography.tractography.estimate_tractography_parameters
documentation:
Estimate parameters for tractography by normalizing them to the
pixel size of the image.

Parameters
----------
in_file : str
    Path to the input file.
stepscale : float
    Step size in mm.
lenscale_min : int
    Minimum length of the tract in mm.
lenscale_max : int
    Maximum length of the tract in mm.

Returns
-------
stepscale : float
    Step size in mm.
lenscale_min : int
    Minimum length of the tract in mm.
lenscale_max : int
    Maximum length of the tract in mm.

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.tractography.tractography.format_algorithm
documentation:
Format the algorithm name.

Parameters
----------
algorithm : str
    the algorithm name

Returns
-------
str
    the formatted algorithm name

documentation:
Build the SDC and motion correction workflow.

Parameters

name : str, optional
    name of the workflow (default: "eddy_wf")

Returns
-------
Workflow
    the workflow

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.snr.calc_snr
documentation:
Calculate the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the diffusion-weighted images.

Parameters
----------
dwi_file : str
    Path to the diffusion-weighted image.
tissue_mask : str
    Path to the tissue mask.
brain_mask : str
    Path to the brain mask.

Returns
-------
snr_values : list
    List of SNR values.

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.procedures.quality_control.snr.tissue_snr_to_csv
documentation:
Convert the SNR values to a CSV file.

Parameters
----------
snr_values : list
    List of SNR values.
tissues : list
    List of tissues.
dwi_bval : list
    List of b-values.
out_file : str
    Path to the output CSV file.

Returns
-------
out_file : str
    Path to the output CSV file.

documentation: Workflow to perform tractography using MRtrix3.

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.diffusion.init_diffusion_wf
documentation:
Initialize the diffusion postprocessing workflow.

Parameters
----------
dwi_data : dict
    The dictionary of data for the diffusion data.
prefix : str, optional
    The name of the workflow, by default "dwi_postprocess"

Returns
-------
Workflow
    The diffusion postprocessing workflow

functions.name: kepost.workflows.diffusion.diffusion._get_wf_name
documentation:
Derive the workflow name for supplied DWI file.
Examples
\--------
\>>> \_get_wf_name("/completely/made/up/path/sub-01_dir-AP_acq-64grad_dwi.nii.gz")
'dwi_preproc_dir_AP_acq_64grad_wf'
\>>> \_get_wf_name("/completely/made/up/path/sub-01_dir-RL_run-01_echo-1_dwi.nii.gz")
'dwi_preproc_dir_RL_run_01_echo_1_wf'
